title: End Session Procedure
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the controlled two-phase process for preparing, reviewing, approving, documenting, and formally closing every TMS-OS work session.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This procedure defines the official process for closing every Two Marshalls Studios work session. It ensures that completed work is tested, documented, reviewed, approved, packaged, and preserved before the session becomes part of the permanent studio record.
The Studio Operating System manages the closing workflow. The studio owner reviews and approves the results. Automation prepares the work but does not assume approval.

sections.title: Scope
paragraphs: This procedure applies to every controlled TMS-OS work session, department, module, milestone, bug fix, production change, and release-related session.

sections.title: Core Operating Principles
items: The Studio Operating System manages the workflow.
The studio owner approves the results.
Session Context is maintained throughout the work session.
Sessions end only at logical checkpoints.
Permanent actions require a Human Approval Gate.
Session closing uses a two-phase Prepare, Review, Approve, and Close process.
Git commit, Git push, version changes, release tags, and final session closure require explicit approval.

sections.title: Session Boundary Management
paragraphs: The assistant is responsible for monitoring progress and identifying logical checkpoints. The studio owner is not expected to remember when to begin the closing process.
items: When a logical checkpoint is reached, the assistant explicitly instructs the studio owner to type Prepare Session.
After the review package is generated, the assistant explicitly instructs the studio owner to choose Approve & Close, Revise, or Cancel.
A session should not normally close in the middle of an untested or incomplete logical unit of work.

sections.title: Required Preconditions
paragraphs: Before the Prepare Session phase begins, the current logical unit of work should be complete enough to review.
items: Verified bugs and fixes are recorded.
Decisions, lessons, risks, technical debt, and enhancement ideas are captured when applicable.
The exact next-session continuation point is known.

sections.title: Phase 1 — Prepare Session
paragraphs: At a logical checkpoint, the assistant tells the studio owner exactly when to type Prepare Session. This command begins preparation only. Nothing is finalized, committed, pushed, tagged, or formally closed during this phase.
items: Record files added, modified, and removed.
Record bugs fixed and known bugs.
Record decisions, lessons, risks, and technical debt.
Record enhancement ideas without implementing unapproved enhancements.
Prepare the session package, README-FIRST.md, Git commit notes, and next-session continuation point.

sections.title: Review Gate
paragraphs: After preparation, the assistant presents the complete review package and explicitly tells the studio owner which command to use.

rows: Approve the prepared package and proceed to formal closure.
Return to active work so the package or project can be corrected.
Stop the closing process and keep the session open without finalizing it.

sections.title: Phase 2 — Approve & Close
paragraphs: Only the exact command Approve & Close authorizes formal session closure. The assistant then finalizes the session records and prepares the approved Git actions.
items: Finalize README-FIRST.md and the session ZIP package.
Finalize the file-change list and test report.
Prepare the approved Git commit message and push instructions.
Mark the session closed only after the approval gate has been satisfied.

sections.title: Session Context Record
paragraphs: A live Session Context must be maintained throughout the work session so the closing package does not depend on reconstructing the session from memory.

rows: Observed pass, fail, warning, or blocked results.
Deferred ideas preserved in the Enhancement Ideas Log.
Open, Preparing, Review, Revised, Cancelled, or Closed.

sections.title: Permanent Documentation Updates
paragraphs: The closing process must determine which permanent documents require an append operation, a current-state replacement, or no change.

rows: Append only when a milestone or checkpoint is completed or materially changed.
Append verified bugs and fixes when applicable.
Append verified reusable lessons when applicable.
Append or update verified technical debt when applicable.
Append deferred enhancement ideas when applicable.
Append or update verified risks when applicable.
Append only when the session is historically significant.
Replace the current-state snapshot with the newly verified state.
Replace the documentation-state snapshot when documentation status changes.
Update when a permanent document is added, renamed, moved, superseded, archived, or verified.

sections.title: Session Package Requirements
items: Installation instructions when files must be replaced

sections.title: Version and Git Control
paragraphs: The closing process may recommend a version number, commit message, release note, branch action, push, or release tag, but it may not treat those recommendations as approved actions.
items: The assistant prepares the proposed Git commit message.
The assistant tells the studio owner exactly when to commit and push.
The studio owner must explicitly approve any commit, push, version change, or tag.
GitHub becomes the shared code checkpoint only after the approved push succeeds.

sections.title: Validation Requirements
paragraphs: Before closure is recommended, the final project state and generated documentation must be validated.
items: StudioDB loads without invalid records or duplicate IDs.
Document catalog and connected content counts are correct.
Browser console contains no new JavaScript errors.
All required manual tests have been performed and recorded.

sections.title: Closure Standard
paragraphs: A work session is officially closed only after the assistant has prepared the review package, the studio owner has entered Approve & Close, required documentation has been finalized, and the resulting Git instructions have been issued.
After closure, the assistant provides the exact next work-session starting point and tells the studio owner when Git commit and push should occur.

sections.title: Verification
paragraphs: This procedure is verified when it is connected to the Documentation Catalog, renders through the Shared Document Template Engine and Embedded Viewer, appears in Shared Search, and the catalog reports the expected permanent document count without JavaScript errors.

rows: Created the permanent End Session Procedure with Session Context, explicit operator commands, two-phase closing, and Human Approval Gate.

requirements: Register PROC-004 in StudioDB permanent document records.
Confirm Documentation Catalog reports 20 documents and 20 connected content files.
Open PROC-004 in the Embedded Viewer.
Verify tables, lists, metadata, and related documents render correctly.
Verify Shared Search finds End Session Procedure, Prepare Session, and Approve & Close.
